title: Interlock
description: Interlock Input at TB3/9 is low or open
causes: 24 V not present on TB3/9. This is usually due to a plant E-stop or interlock being open
action: Check for open E-stops or interlocks outside the drive cubicle. Check that TB3 is not loose.

title: Reference Loss
description: No reference source can be found (either primary or backup)
causes: The drive receives references from analogue inputs, 4-20mA etc or Fieldbus link and the active reference has gone
action: Check more trip codes on Menu 10 by scrolling through. Extra trip codes will give clues to which reference has been lost.

title: DC Overvolts
description: DC Link Voltage exceeds the over voltage level: 780 V on a 380V-480VAC system; 875V on a 525VAC system; 1165V on a 690VAC system
causes: The DC Link volts monitored on P11.03 has risen; usually caused by the motor decelerating quickly and regenerating back into the drive. Check decelerating ramp rates are not too high or that any dynamic brake fitted is operating
action: If the decelerating ramp rate that stops the drive from tripping is too long, fit a DB. This is not a drive fault but an application error. Refer to Over/Under volts trip table in the appropriate product technical manual

title: DC Undervolts
description: DC Link Voltage is less than the under voltage level: 400V on a 380V-480VAC system; 450V on a 525VAC system; 560V on a 690VAC system
action: When a MicroCubicle™ CDC or a DELTA CDC is operating from 24 V auxiliaries the mains power is off and so the DC Link is not established.

title: Timed Over-current
description: Total I.T limit of the drive overload has been exceeded

title: Over-temperature -see P45.22 (pointed at by P10.10)
description: An Over-temperature trip has occurred - see P45.22 for more details
causes: Over temperature trips are generated by software in the CDC, by comparing temperature feedback to trip levels, or in the SKiiP® circuits in hardware, in case the software fails: - the temperature feedback is high - check Menu 45; - the drive ventilation is inhibited - check temperature, check air throughput against table in specification section of the appropriate product manual; - for liquid cooled products check that the coolant flow rates and volumes meet the specification in the product manual.
action: Refer to over temperature trips at Table 6-11. Menu 45 is dedicated to temperature feedback - some of the temperatures are also included at Menu 11. When a MicroCubicle is operating from 28V auxiliaries the SKiiP circuits are not powered up, which is where the trip originates, and the drive will show this trip.

title: Instantaneous Over-current
description:
The drive output current has exceeded its maximum safe level – see diagram below for an indication of various trip levels.

Note: When a Fault Code 7 occurs it is also likely that there will be a fault on any 8 to 13 U, V, W trip codes.
causes: The current feedback has exceeded the trip levels within the product: - motor faulty; - motor cable damage; - shock loads to system - however, the drive's internal current clipping ride-through function should stop this; - possible faulty transistors: - remove the motor cables, if the drive still trips then it is at fault internally; - for a DELTA system try exchanging the ribbons in the CDC and see if the trip 'moves'. If it does, the DELTA now reporting the trip is faulty. If it doesn't the CDC is faulty.
action: Refer to diagram under 'Description' for the various trip levels. When operating from 28V the drive will trip because the SKiiP circuits, within the product, are not powered. The exact list of trips will depend on the size of the drive powered by 28V.

title: Instantaneous Over-current on U-phase
description: U-phase transistor turned off by self-protection*

title: Hardware Over-temperature on U-phase
description: Over-temperature detected by U-phase output*

title: Instantaneous Over-current on V-phase
description: V-phase transistor turned off by self-protection*

title: Hardware Over-temperature on V-phase
description: Over-temperature detected by V-phase output*

title: Instantaneous Over-current on W-phase
description: W-phase transistor turned off by self-protection*

title: Hardware Over-temperature on W-phase
description: Over-temperature detected by W-phase output*
causes: The Over temperature Trips in Code 5 should react first. However, if the CDC is not functioning then the temperature may keep rising. The SKiiP® circuits have a hardware temperature monitor for safety and these may trigger - is the drive really hot - check Menus 11 and 45 - if YES check fans and ventilation requirements - if NO then the drive may be at fault - e.g. change ribbon cables on DELTAs.
action: When operating from 24 V the drive will trip because the SKiiP® circuits, within the product, are not powered. The exact list of trips will depend on the size of the drive powered by 24 V.

title: Encoder Power Supply Fail
description: Encoder supply is internally supplied at 8 V then regulated down to the value set in P13.06 and 'I' limited, then if current flowing to the encoder is too high, trip here.
causes: Check encoder cabling for damage - remove encoder connection at TB3 and try a reset to see if drive or cable is at fault - is encoder correct type? Refer to 'Encoder Selection' in appropriate product manual.

title: Controller 15 V Fail
description: Internal supply voltage on the Control Board has failed. Failure of 15 V supply could result in: (a) no +15V for SKiiP which will result in any number of trips on the SKiiP module e.g. over temperature, over current.
causes: Faulty CDC

title: 24 V Output Fail
description: The User +24 V output from the Control Electronics has failed, or been overloaded.
causes: Disconnect User I/O terminals from the drive. See if 24 V recovers - check wiring if it does.

title: Unidentified Power Interface Board
description: The drive cannot recognise the Power Interface (PIB) PCB, and therefore cannot run. It looks for an E2PROM on the PIB.
causes: E2PROM failure on a MicroCubicle™ - the PIB is part of the CDC. On a DELTA system the PIB is within the controller's metal case.

title: History Restore Fail
description: The Non-volatile History record cannot be recovered at power-up.
causes: Can happen after a firmware upgrade and is OK. Is not critical if history record is not needed and therefore it can be ignored.

title: New PCB
description: One of the printed circuit boards (pcb) in the drive has changed from when the drive was last switched off. Refer to Spares Manual supplied with new pcb.
causes: Exchanging pcbs between drives; Changing DELTAs; Changing DELTA pcbs. It is important that the correct spares procedures are followed.

title: Parameter Edits Lost
description: The Non-volatile Parameter Edits cannot be recovered at power-up. All parameters have returned to their factory default values.
causes: This trip is rare and is a warning. The DC Link on the drive may have been dragged down very quickly, preventing enough time for parameter storage on Power Off. Check for excessive DC Link Loads e.g. faulty DBs or DC Link schemes faulty.

title: Motor Thermostat (MTRIP)
description: Motor thermostat has tripped. This trip can only occur if the thermostat is connected via a digital input to CF1.13 and P2.10 = 2. See Control Block Diagram Sheet 7.
causes: CF1.13 is healthy when High, so n/c thermostats need to be inverted by placing a -ve sign in the edit.
action: Check motor fans and thermostat functionality.

title: Motor I²T
description: The motor I²T limit has been exceeded. This trip can only occur if P2.09 = 2.
causes: Is the motor free to rotate? Is the motor sized correctly for the load? Is P2.07 set correctly? Is P2.08 set correctly?

title: RS232 Loss
description: RS232 serial link has timed out. This trip can only occur if P32.15 = 2.
causes: Check RS232 connections and wiring. Is the serial link manager plc OK? Check that the time-out period is set correctly.

title: RS485 Loss
description: RS485 serial link has timed out. This trip can only occur if P32.35 = 2.
causes: Check RS485 connections and wiring. Is the serial link manager plc OK? Check that the time-out period is set correctly.

title: Internal Reference Fail
description: Internal reference voltage has failed, CDC faulty
causes: The CDC is faulty - replace.

title: Under temperature - see P45.22
description: An under temperature trip has occurred - see P45.22 for more details
causes: Is the temperature actually low? See Menu 11 and 45. If it is the drive cannot operate <0°C. Temperature feedback comes from the SKiiP® Module. Check connections to the CDC.

title: Keypad Loss/Removed
description: Keypad Communications have illegally halted. See Keypad removal at 2.2.5.
causes: The Keypad has been removed when it was either in control (Start/Stop) or had reference control (Keypad ref).

title: Current Imbalance
description: The 3 output phases are imbalanced in current. This trip can only occur in VVF mode (P99.01 = 1)
causes: If the current is different by 20% in any 1 phase the drive trips. Disconnect motor cables and see if the trip remains: - if YES then = drive; - if NO then = motor; Check motor. Check motor cables.
action: Check fans and ventilation. Check motor loads and cables - can disconnect motor cables and run drives to see if trip goes away - if it does the drive is not at fault.

title: Precharge failure
description: The Power Interface Board received no pre-charge contactor close signal. On a DELTA, check the rectifier wiring.
causes: 24 V Precharge Acknowledge on PL12/9 has not been detected - wiring error or relay on DELTA Rectifier faulty or 24 V is down.
action: Check fans and ventilation. Check motor loads and cables - can disconnect motor cables and run drives to see if trip goes away - if it does the drive is not at fault.

title: Drive ID Violation
description: P99.13 is not equal to P99.14
causes: Simple editing error. FIP or Fieldbus parameter set sent to wrong drive.
action: Check fans and ventilation. Check motor loads and cables - can disconnect motor cables and run drives to see if trip goes away - if it does the drive is not at fault.

title: DELTA 1 Over-current on U-phase
description: U-phase transistor on DELTA 1 turned off by self-protection.
causes:
The Over current and Over temperature trips are operated by the SKiiP® Modules. Either the temperature or current is high (check Menus 11 and 45) or the SKiiP® Modules are faulty (move CDC ribbons for the DELTAs to see if the Trips move) or the controller is faulty. If the trips do not move with the DELTA ribbons and not the motor then it must be the controller.

Checking if drive is faulty:
1. Remove motor cables from drive end;
2. Run the drive in VVF Mode and see if trips occur - if not drive is OK - if it does it may be controller or DELTA at fault.

Checking if DELTA is faulty:
1. With the power OFF, move DELTA ribbons in the DELTA CDC;
2. Power ON and run the drive.
3. If the drive trips again and the trip has moved with the DELTA then the DELTA is at fault.

If the drive trips on the same trip then the DELTA is OK but the CDC is at fault.
action: Check fans and ventilation. Check motor loads and cables - can disconnect motor cables and run drives to see if trip goes away - if it does the drive is not at fault.

title: DELTA 1 External Trip
description: External trip detected on DELTA 1, TB1.
causes: Only possible on Liquid Cooled DELTA (LCD) - this TB is used to detect fan failure on the LCD. See DC Link Overvoltage Trip 3. Could be faulty SMPS - exchange with another.

title: DELTA 1 Over-voltage
description: DC Link over voltage detected on DELTA 1 - signal from the SMPS

title: DELTA 1 Enhanced DIB Trip
description: Reported if an enhanced trip from new Delta Interface Board (20X4381) is detected by an older controller.
causes: Controllers made after September 2003 show trip codes 240 to 249 instead.

1. With the power OFF, move DELTA ribbons in the DELTA CDC;
2. Power ON and run the drive.
3. If the drive trips again and the trip has moved with the DELTA then the DELTA is at fault.

If the drive trips on the same trip then the DELTA is OK but the CDC is at fault.

title: DELTA 3 External Trip
description: External trip detected on DELTA 3, TB1.
causes: Only possible on Liquid Cooled DELTA (LCD) - this TB is used to detect fan failure on the LCD. See DC Link Overvoltage Trip 3. Could be faulty SMPS - exchange with another.

title: DELTA 3 Over-voltage
description: DC Link over voltage detected on DELTA 3

title: Overspeed
description: The motor is operating above its set over speed level. This trip can only occur if P29.02 = 1.
causes: Speed feedback level in P9.01 is > over speed level in P29.00, P29.01. Is the encoder OK? Is the motor being overhauled? If in torque control, the motor may be running off due to mechanical failure of the load.

title: Current Control Fault
description: Drive cannot control the motor current as required. Check the motor model, mains voltage, encoder connections and check for earth faults.
causes: Value set in P12.02 is too high, or mains supply voltage is lower than thought. Poor motor model - do a CAL run. Encoder connections - mechanical and electrical are poor. Ensure the motor is off-load - the drive checks this at the beginning of the test. Motor parameters have been entered manually and are incorrect - do a CAL run.
action: Are the PID settings for Speed Amplifier OK? It may be unstable.

title: Motor Calibration Failure
description: Motor Calibration Test (P3.12 or 12.03 = 3) failed. Check the motor and encoder connections.
action: Motor must be off load.

title: Unsuitable Motor
description: The Motor Parameters entered do not describe a motor within the control capabilities of the drive.

title: Encoder Loss
description: Encoder Signal has been lost, or cannot be relied upon. This trip can only occur if P13.02 = 1. See Control Block Diagram Sheet 6.
causes: The drive checks for three conditions to flag encoder loss: Maximum speed change (set in P13.02); Maximum reversals (set in P13.19); Reversible threshold (set in P13.20). Check encoder electrical and mechanical connections - ensure encoder cable has a continuous screen and is earthed at both ends.

title: User Trip 1
description: Control Flag 10 = 1
causes: CF10 has been connected to something by the user - ensure that whatever it is connected to is healthy (i.e. producing a LO on CF10).

title: Fieldbus Loss
description: The Fieldbus communication link has failed.
causes: FIP, PROFIBUS, are Fieldbus communication links - the one in use has failed.

title: Load Fault - High
description: The drive has detected a load fault due to excessive load.
causes: Menu 43 allows the drive to monitor the load - check mechanics for bearing failure or coupling breakages etc.

title: Load Fault - Low
description: The drive has detected a load fault due to inadequate load.

title: Motor PTC Trip
description: Excessive temperature detected by PTC on TB5, pin 1. See Menu 2.
causes: Is the motor stalled? Is the motor fan faulty? Is the motor PTC faulty? Is the PTC wiring and connectors OK?

title: DB resistor trip
description: Drive estimates the DB resistor is too hot.
causes: Only possible for a MV DB. Drive bases estimation on information typed into P23.01 to P23.03.

title: Unknown Drive Size
description: The software was unable to read the E2PROM on one, or more, of the pcbs.
causes: E2PROM's exist on the SMPS, the DELTA PIBs and the DELTA DIBs.

title: Configuration Trip
description: The drive is not correctly configured to operate in a DELTA system. DELTAs are not connected to the DELTA Controller correctly.
causes: The DELTAs must be plugged in from DELTA 1 slot to DELTA 6 with no gaps; The DELTAs must all be the same type; The SMPS units on the DELTAs must all be the same type.

title: Datumize Error
description: Datumizing (Position Control Only) encountered limit switches in the wrong order. See Position Control
causes: Ensure that the HI and LO position limit switches are wired and programmed correctly to CF87 and CF86. Datumizing speeds set in P36.35 to P36.37 are too high – see Datumizing 6.16.3.

title: Speed Feedback Loss
description: Only applies to Speed Feedback Vector Control Mode (P99.01 = 2, and P13.00 = 1 & 2)
causes: The selected speed feedback is no longer present, or backup has been selected, and it has not been programmed or connected.

title: Over Frequency
description: Allowed output frequency has been exceeded. 250Hz at switching frequencies >= 2.5kHz; 125Hz at switching frequency = 1.25kHz
causes: Absolute limit to prevent IGBTs from being damaged.

title: User Trip 2
description: Control Flag 112 = 1
causes: See User Trip 1 to Trip Code 62.

title: DELTA 3 Over-current
description: Reported if an enhanced trip from new Delta Interface Board (20X4381) is detected by an older controller.
causes: Controllers made after September 2005 show trip codes 260 to 269 instead.

title: DELTA 4 Over-current on U-phase
description: U-phase transistor on DELTA 4 turned off by self-protection
causes:
Checks for Overcurrent on DELTA 4 are the same as those for DELTA 1 - The Over current and Over temperature trips are operated by the SKiiP® Modules. Either the temperature or current is high (check Menus 11 and 45) or the SKiiP® Modules are faulty (move CDC ribbons for the DELTAs to see if the Trips move) or the controller is faulty. If the trips do not move with the DELTA ribbons and not the motor then it must be the controller.

Checking if drive is faulty:
1. Remove motor cables from drive end;
2. Run the drive in VVF Mode and see if trips occur - if not drive is OK - if it does it may be controller or DELTA at fault.

Checking if DELTA is faulty:
1. With the power OFF, move DELTA ribbons in the DELTA CDC;
2. Power ON and run the drive.
3. If the drive trips again and the trip has moved with the DELTA then the DELTA is at fault.

If the drive trips on the same trip then the DELTA is OK but the CDC is at fault.

title: SFE Mains frequency detected outside of limits
description: Mains frequency has been detected outside of the limits set by P52.08 and P52.11
causes: The Mains Monitor Unit may not be connected correctly. The mains frequency may be varying.

title: SFE Synchronisation to mains has failed
description: Synchronisation to the mains has failed due to: a) permanent loss of mains signal or b) temporary interruption of mains signal or c) mains frequency has changed at a rate greater than 2%/s.
causes: The Mains Monitor Unit may not be connected at all, or connected correctly.

title: SFE Mains Voltage Monitor Loss
description: Mains Voltage Monitor Loss
causes: A ribbon cable may have become disconnected

title: SFE Auxiliary Phase Detection Loss
description: Auxiliary Phase Detection Loss
causes: There may be a missing phase on an Input Monitor or a mismatch of amplitude and phasing on the incoming phases.

title: Pre-release Expiry
description: Pre-release Software time limit expired
causes: A pre-release version of firmware has been left in the drive - update to latest version.

title: SFE Choke ptc trip
description: Choke PTC resistance is greater than the threshold set in P52.19 and P52.20 is set to the value 2.
causes: The SFE Choke is either very hot; Or its detector (PTC) has failed; Or its detector (PTC) is not wired correctly.

title: Excess output current
description: Excess output current
causes: Drive output current > drive continuous rating

causes:
The controller has detected an error in the PWM Pattern. CDC is at fault. 
Check DB resistor wiring, or DB resistor, maybe DB Family. 
Check DB resistor wiring and resistor. The DB may be hot due to short circuit of cables or DB may be faulty. 
Checks for Overcurrent on DELTA 6 are the same as those for DELTA 1 - refer to details at Fault Code 31. 
Checks for over temperature on DELTA 6 are the same as those for DELTA 1 - refer to details at Fault Code 31.

title: DELTA 6 External trip
description: External trip detected on DELTA 6, TB1.
causes: Only possible on Liquid Cooled DELTA (LCD) - this TB is used to detect fan failure on the LCD. See DC Link Overvoltage Trip 3. Could be faulty SMPS - exchange with another.

title: DELTA 6 Over-voltage
description: DC Link over voltage detected on DELTA 6.

title: DELTA 6 Over-current
description: Reported if an enhanced trip from new Delta Interface Board (20X4381) is detected by an older controller.
causes: Controllers made after September 2005 show trip codes 290 to 299 instead.

title: DB 1 Over-current U
description: U-phase transistor on DB 1 turned off by self-protection.
causes: DELTA Bridges can be used as DBs by connecting them into the relevant connections or the CDC Controller; the trips are therefore generated in the same way as a normal DELTA, so refer to DELTA 1 Codes. However, over currents can be caused by faulty resistors, or resistor wiring, as there is no motor here - check wiring and resistors for damage. Move the ribbons from DB1 to DB2. See if the trip moves with the DB or the Controller.

title: PIB FPGA config. Failure
description: Internal failure. Report to GE Power Conversion
causes: Identity in E²PROM on MicroCubicle™ SMPS is unrecognisable - faulty SMPS or corrupt E²PROM or maybe loose ribbon connections between CDC and SMPS.

title: Unidentified SMPS
description: System error as described. Check wiring.

title: Unidentified DELTA 1
description: Identity information in DIB is unrecognisable.

title: Unidentified SMPS 1
description: Identity information in DELTA SMPS is unrecognisable.
action: When a DELTA CDC is operating from 24 V auxiliaries the CDC is unable to read the E²PROM on the DELTA SMPS (Switched Mode Power Supply) so this trip will occur. The trip can be masked by CF108.

title: Incorrect DELTA 1
description: The complete DELTA is incorrect type
causes: This means that the SMPS and the DELTA are not compatible. There may be a high voltage SMPS with a low voltage DELTA or vice versa - check correct SMPS type for DELTA.
action: When a DELTA CDC is operating from 24 V auxiliaries the CDC is unable to read the E²PROM on the DELTA SMPS (Switched Mode Power Supply) so this trip will occur. The trip can be masked by CF108.

title: Unidentified DELTA 2
description: Identity information in DIB is unrecognisable.
causes: Identity information in DIB is unrecognisable.
action: When a DELTA CDC is operating from 24 V auxiliaries the CDC is unable to read the E²PROM on the DELTA DIB (Delta Interface Board) so this trip will occur.

title: Unidentified SMPS 2
description: Identity information in DELTA SMPS is unrecognisable.
action: When a DELTA CDC is operating from 24 V auxiliaries the CDC is unable to read the E²PROM on the DELTA SMPS (Switched Mode Power Supply).

title: CAN 1 Loss, see P61.43
description: CAN is not communicating correctly
causes: Some possible causes of a trip are: CAN cable disconnected; No other active nodes on the CAN Network. Refer to T2013 CANopen Fieldbus Facility Technical Manual for more details.

title: Unsuitable DB Load
description: Load too high (resistor value too low) on DB mode, on IGBT Bridge
causes: When using a Power Bridge as a DB (P23.19 = 1) incorrect value of DB resistors will trigger this trip. Check resistor values; Check DB resistor wiring.

title: DELTA Rectifier Thermostat Trip
description: Thermostat connections (PL12/3 and PL12/4) on DELTA Controller are open circuit.
causes: Missing or damaged wiring between DELTA Controller (PL12/3/4) and DELTA Rectifier (TB1/3/4).

title: CAN 2 LOSS See P65.43
description: As Trip 200
causes: As Trip 200

title: Fieldbus Coupler 1 Loss
description: The Fieldbus communication associated with Menu 74 has failed
causes: Cable fallen out. Not configured correctly - either on drive or network master.

title: U-Phase Sharing Failure
description: Imbalance between DELTA U-Phase Currents
causes: The U- Phases between all of the connected DELTA units are not sharing current equally: Unequal wiring impedances/lengths; Loose wiring terminals; Faulty DELTA unit

title: V-Phase Sharing Failure
description: Imbalance between DELTA V-Phase Currents
causes: As Trip 207, except for V-Phase

title: W-Phase Sharing Failure
description: Imbalance between DELTA W-Phase Currents
causes: As Trip 207, except for W-Phase

title: Mains Amplitude Trip
description: The mains supply amplitude has been outside the limits set by P52.22 and P52.23 for longer than the time set by P52.24

title: Not 40MHz CDC
description: The firmware fitted is only compatible with a 40MHz CDC and the CDC in use is an older 25MHz version

title: Undiagnosed Trip DELTA 1
description: Controller has detected a trip from DELTA 1 but is unable to diagnose details of the trip.
causes: High amounts of electrical noise. Poor earthing. Incorrect screening of ribbon cables.

title: Undiagnosed Trip DELTA 2
description: Controller has detected a trip from DELTA 1 but is unable to diagnose details of the trip.
action: Later Delta Interface Boards (20X4381 or 20X4384) should fix this problem.
